channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump says he will offer 'gold cards' for $5 million path to citizenship, replacing investor visas
item.description: President Donald Trump said Tuesday that he plans to offer a “gold card” visa with a path to citizenship for $5 million, replacing a 35-year-old visa for investors.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ukraine and US have agreed on a framework economic deal, Ukrainian officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine and the U.S. have reached an agreement on a framework for a broad economic deal that would include access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals, three senior Ukrainian officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, HANNA ARHIROVA and VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Southwest Airlines flight abruptly rises to avoid another plane crossing Chicago runway
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Pilots on a Southwest Airlines flight attempting to land at Chicago's Midway Airport were forced to climb back into the sky to avoid another aircraft crossing the runway on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS and KATHLEEN FOODY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Another $500M for Hurricane Helene relief in North Carolina passes key hurdle
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Another $500 million would be spent toward Hurricane Helene recovery efforts in western North Carolina in legislation the state House unanimously approved Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Missouri woman accused in plot to sell Graceland says she'll plead guilty
item.description: A Missouri woman said Tuesday she is pleading guilty to a federal charge accusing her of concocting a brazen plot to defraud Elvis Presley’s family by trying to auction off his Graceland mansion and property before a judge halted the mysterious foreclosure sale.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump moves to suspend security clearances of lawyers at DC law firm helping Jack Smith
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday ordered the suspension of any security clearances held by lawyers at a prominent Washington law firm who provided legal services to special counsel Jack Smith, the latest move in the Republican's campaign of retribution over the criminal investigat
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dragons to fly over Universal’s ‘How To Train Your Dragon’ land
item.description: Walking and flying dragons will greet guests as they explore How to Train Your Dragon – Isle of Berk, one of the five immersive lands that will be featured in the all-new Universal Epic Universe set to open on May 22. The theme park resort teased the news in a blog post released Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump suspends security clearances for Jack Smith's outside lawyers
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday signed a memo suspending security clearances for a number of outside lawyers who assisted with former special counsel Jack Smith’s investigation into the now-president. White House staff secretary Will Scharf said in the Oval Office that the memo targeted Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Over 20 staffers in office overtaken by DOGE resign
item.description: The staffers said they won’t use their technical skills to “jeopardize” Americans’ data and “compromise” essential government systems.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: VA axes another 1,400 employees
item.description: The Department of Veterans Affairs on Monday fired another 1,400 employees amid outcry over a lack of transparency from the agency after 1,000 workers were axed earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: SWAT team raided the wrong Denver apartment and traumatized two young girls, lawsuit says
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Kirsty Shelton was at home preparing lunch and watching television with her daughters and their grandmother when they were startled by loud banging on their apartment door by a SWAT team looking for a man who lived across the hall.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man arrested for allegedly saying he was 'going to burn down' xAI facility
item.description: A man was arrested after allegedly saying he was "going to burn down" the xAI supercomputer facility in Memphis.
item.dc:creator: Andy Cordan, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court orders new trial for Oklahoma death row inmate
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday sided with an Oklahoma death row inmate who claimed alongside the state itself that his trial was unfair, ordering a new trial and staving off his execution.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Colorado puppy thieves fake seizure to steal dogs from pet store in heist caught on video: police
item.description: Colorado puppy thieves faked a seizure to create a distraction during a heist at a Denver-area pet store that was captured on video, police say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What will win at a nailbiter Oscars? AP's film writers make their predictions
item.description: By the time the Oscars wind around after months of other award shows and constant handicapping, the prizes can feel almost predetermined. Not this year.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE and LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: United Airlines flight makes emergency landing at Newark Airport
item.description: NEWARK, New Jersey (PIX11) -- A United Airlines flight made an emergency landing at Newark Liberty International Airport on Tuesday morning, according to Port Authority officials. United Flight 1544 took off from Newark Airport just before 6 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, NewsNation

item.title: Why you won't receive a Supplemental Security Income check in March
item.description: It's the latest blip in the payment schedule but don't worry — beneficiaries will still receive all of their payments.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Student loan borrowers face abrupt change as GOP budget plans threaten to raise payments
item.description: Student loan borrowers could see their payments increase under budget plans currently under discussion by House Republicans, a violent about-face after years of working with the Biden administration to lower payments and secure debt relief.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: No savings expected from nearly 40% of DOGE-canceled contracts
item.description: Nearly 40% of the federal contracts that the Trump administration claims to have canceled as part of its signature cost-cutting program aren’t expected to save the government any money, the administration's own data shows.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pope Francis is critical but stable with no new respiratory crises: Vatican
item.description: “In the morning, after receiving the Eucharist, he resumed work activities,” the Vatican statement said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Musk says failure to respond to email a second time will end in termination
item.description: The message appears to contradict recent guidance from the Office of Personnel Management (OPM), which told agency leaders Monday afternoon that employee response to the initial email was not mandatory and that failure to do so would not be considered a resignation.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump backs Musk as he roils the federal workforce with demands and threats
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump backed Elon Musk's demand that federal employees explain their recent accomplishments by the end of Monday or risk getting fired, even as government agency officials were told that compliance with Musk's edict was voluntary.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Antivirus software company ordered to pay customer $16.5M: How to file a claim
item.description: Millions of American consumers who bought antivirus software from Avast may be eligible to receive a portion of a $16.5 million settlement, the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: McConnell suggests Trump has ‘gross misunderstanding’ of Ukraine talks
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) on Monday suggested that President Trump’s refusal to blame Russia as the sole aggressor who provoked the war in Ukraine “reflects a gross misunderstanding of the nature of negotiations and leverage.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: Delta flight returns to Atlanta after possible smoke reported in flightdeck
item.description: A Delta flight returned to Atlanta on Monday after the crew observed a “haze” in the aircraft shortly after takeoff, the airline said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Trump defends Musk’s ‘genius’ email to federal workers
item.description: President Trump on Monday defended tech billionaire Elon Musk after pushback over his email demanding federal workers list five accomplishments from the past week and in doing so, raising the of risk losing their jobs. “There was a lot of genius in sending it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Delta flight forced to return to Atlanta airport after 'haze' fills cabin
item.description: A Delta Air Lines flight was forced to return to Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport on Monday after a "haze" filled the cabin, the airline said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Key federal agencies refuse to comply with Musk's latest demand in his cost-cutting crusade
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Key U.S. agencies, including the FBI, State Department and the Pentagon, have instructed their employees not to comply with cost-cutting chief Elon Musk's latest demand that federal workers explain what they accomplished last week — or risk losing their job.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, ERIC TUCKER and AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, ABC 4
